steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure at the boundary
steps.content: To the left of the blank: “two varieties of the sweet potato”—a general noun phrase naming a category. To the right: “TN57, which is known for its insect resistance, and TN66, which is much more susceptible to pests”—a list of specific items that clarify what those two varieties are.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces a list or elaboration. Here, the specific varieties TN57 and TN66 elaborate on what “two varieties of the sweet potato” means.

A period creates a fragment, a semicolon is for independent clauses (not for lists of names), and no punctuation is non-standard.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
